privacy.title: Privacy and Rules
privacy.meta: How TinyCat handles accounts, public content, recovery hashes, reports, limits, and content moderation.
privacy.intro: TinyCat is an open and free public social platform designed not to require a real-world identity. This overview explains what is public, what the app stores, how password recovery works, and how reported content is handled.
privacy.public_open: TinyCat is intended for free public sharing. Posts, comments, profiles, likes, shares, follows, and tags are public parts of the platform.
privacy.public_content: Content may be visible to other users and visitors without signing in, and may be available through public links, search, profiles, tags, or shares.
privacy.public_no_private: The platform has no private messages, private profiles, or personal storage mode. TinyCat is therefore not meant for sharing private or sensitive things.
privacy.public_no_personal: Do not share personal data, intimate content, non-public documents, or anything you do not want to be publicly accessible.
privacy.data_account: Registration does not require contact information or a real name. The primary identifier is the username.
privacy.data_content: The app stores content users create themselves: posts, comments, likes, shares, follows, reports, and optional profile fields.
privacy.data_technical: Passwords are stored only as hashes. The recovery hash is a private security value and the user must store it outside the platform.
privacy.data_server_logs: TinyCat does not use IP tracking or analytics profiles in the application database. The server operator may still have technical server logs outside the app.
privacy.recovery_hash: The only password recovery mechanism is the /recovery page and the private recovery hash.
privacy.recovery_no_contact: The platform does not use contact information for password recovery.
privacy.recovery_lost: If a user loses both the password and the recovery hash, the app has no safe way to restore the account.
privacy.recovery_rotation: After a successful password recovery, the recovery hash is regenerated so the old hash cannot be reused.
privacy.reporting_how: A post can be reported through the report icon on the post. The user selects a reason and may add a note.
privacy.reporting_duplicate: The same user cannot keep reopening the same report. If a post was already reviewed as acceptable, another identical report does not reopen it.
privacy.reporting_result: Multiple reports for the same post are grouped in administration, so moderation handles one post as one case.
privacy.reporting_notification: A user who reported a post gets a notification when the report is resolved or dismissed.
privacy.moderation_right: The platform reserves the right to moderate content for service safety and applicable law.
privacy.moderation_actions: A moderator can keep a post and lock it against edits, hide it, or remove it.
privacy.moderation_mute: If a post is confirmed as harmful, its author may receive a temporary mute, typically for 24 hours.
privacy.moderation_domains: The platform may block sharing specific domains, for example because of spam, malware, or illegal content.
privacy.limits_intro: Limits protect the platform from spam and heavy automated use. Signed-in limits are account-based, without IP tracking. Guest search uses only the technical session and captcha.
privacy.cookies_session: The app uses technical cookies for sessions, CSRF protection, and security captcha.
privacy.cookies_remember: Optional remember-me login uses a technical cookie only when the user enables it.
privacy.cookies_no_ads: TinyCat core does not use advertising cookies or analytics tracking.

maintenance.title: Maintenance
maintenance.intro: Normalize stored links and remove technical rows that no longer help the app.
maintenance.no_results: No cleanup has been run in this session.
maintenance.production_intro: These tasks are safe to run repeatedly after moving TinyCat to another server or after heavier traffic.
maintenance.production_normalize: Canonical links are merged instead of stored again.
maintenance.production_repeatable: Cleanup can be repeated without manual SQL.
maintenance.production_no_ip: No IP tracking is used by these tasks.
maintenance.production_batched: Large deletes run in small batches to avoid PHP timeouts.

tasks.normalize_links_help: Canonicalizes URLs, merges duplicate social and web links, and keeps the richest metadata.
tasks.empty_web_links_help: Deletes web preview rows without a title, description, or image.
tasks.orphan_links_help: Deletes links that are not connected to any post.
tasks.orphan_tag_relations_help: Deletes tag relations pointing to missing posts or missing tags.
tasks.orphan_terms_help: Deletes tags that are not connected to any post.
tasks.old_action_limits_help: Deletes account action-limit buckets older than 30 days.
tasks.old_read_notifications_help: Deletes read notifications older than 90 days.

messages.batch_done: Cleanup batch finished. {count} rows were changed, {remaining} still remain.
messages.done_with_errors: Cleanup finished with errors. {count} rows were changed.
messages.nothing_selected: Select at least one cleanup task.

install.title: Install TinyCat CMS
install.tables_intro: TinyCat will create core tables: users, content, terms, content_tags, links, content_links, content_shares, content_reactions, content_comments, comment_likes, user_followers, notifications, and settings.
install.purpose_links: Unique links, sources, and preview metadata.
install.purpose_content_links: Links between posts and saved links.
install.purpose_notifications: Alerts for reactions, comments, and shares.
install.purpose_user_action_limits: Account-based action limits without IP tracking.
install.done_intro: TinyCat CMS has a language, database connection, core tables, and first admin account.
